title: Home thread agent self-titling (CLI + system-prompt + contract)
prompt:
GOAL: Make the home (sidebar) chat threads self-title. When the user creates a thread they type a DESCRIPTION (which becomes the thread's kickoff prompt); the thread's OWN agent then summarizes a concise title and sets it via a CLI command, and updates it when the conversation's topic meaningfully shifts. NO separate background summarizer / no completeSimple model call — the conversational agent does it itself in its normal turn.

This is the BACKEND/CLI/contract half. A separate linked task does the web-ui dialog/thread-bar.

CONTEXT (read first):
- src/session/home-thread-store.ts (HomeThreadStore.create/rename) + home-thread-registry.ts (pure ops). create() currently takes { name, agentId }; threadId = randomUUID().
- src/core/home-agent-session.ts (synthetic id __home_agent__:<workspaceId>:<agentId>[:<threadId>]; default thread omits the 4th segment — preserve backward-compat).
- src/terminal/hook-runtime-context.ts — KANBAN_SESSION_TASK_ID_ENV / KANBAN_SESSION_WORKSPACE_ID_ENV are ALREADY injected into the agent session env, so the agent knows its own thread id + workspace id.
- The append-system-prompt mechanism (src/terminal/agent-session-adapters.ts + the runtime vault-types index; memory note 'vault-types-skill-prompt-step3-implemented') — how extra instructions are appended to the agent's system prompt.
- tRPC createHomeThread/renameHomeThread + the RuntimeHomeChatThread* contract in src/core/api-contract.ts.
- CLI command structure: src/commands/*.ts + the runCliCommand envelope seam (memory 'cli-redesign-*'). Follow the positional-id + envelope conventions.

REQUIRED WORK:
1. NEW CLI command for an agent to set its own thread's title, e.g. 'kanban home-thread set-title "<title>"'. Resolve the thread/session id from the KANBAN_SESSION_TASK_ID env automatically (also accept an explicit positional/flag override + --project-path/workspace from KANBAN_SESSION_WORKSPACE_ID). Route to the existing renameHomeThread store op so it persists to threads.json and flows over the existing broadcast. Goes through runCliCommand → envelope like other CLI commands.
2. Add a 'titleSource: "auto" | "manual"' (or titleAuto boolean) field to the thread record/contract. The set-title CLI writes source=auto. A USER rename via the existing rename path sets source=manual and PINS the title (agent set-title must NOT overwrite a manually-set title). Backward-compat: existing threads with no field load as 'manual' (their names were user-typed — never auto-overwrite them).
3. createHomeThread accepts a 'description' (the kickoff prompt) instead of requiring a human title; on create, persist a PROVISIONAL title (truncated/cleaned description) and start the thread's agent session with the description as the first message. The provisional title is source=auto so the agent can replace it.
4. Append a system-prompt instruction (via the existing append mechanism, scoped to the home/sidebar agent sessions) telling the agent: at the start of the conversation, summarize a concise 3-6 word title and set it with 'kanban home-thread set-title'; update it when the topic meaningfully shifts; do not touch it if the user has manually renamed.

OUT OF SCOPE: web-ui create dialog + thread-bar rendering (linked task). But DO update api-contract.ts so the frontend can send a description and read titleSource + the provisional title.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A thread created from a description gets a concise title set by its own agent shortly after the first turn, with no separate summarizer model call.
- Agent re-titles on topic shift; a manual rename pins the title and the agent stops overwriting it.
- New 'kanban home-thread set-title' works from inside an agent session using only the injected env, and returns the standard envelope.
- Backward-compat preserved for the default-thread id scheme + existing threads.json.
- typecheck (incl. web:typecheck for the shared contract) + biome clean. No any types.
